title: dns: reject '/' '*' and other non-LDH chars in dns.lookup hostname
author: robobun
reason: Body shows a `host/path` string "becomes a real QNAME on the wire" and `http.get({ host: "h.invalid/smug" ...})` reaches the origin — hostname validation gap enabling DNS/Host-header injection (SSRF/request-smuggling vector).
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-aarch64 test lane failed, all others pass)

title: FileSystemRouter: do not let percent-decoding change URL structure
author: robobun
reason: Body states "security-adjacent: an encoded / in a single URL segment could select a nested static route the author never exposed to that traffic" — a percent-decoding route bypass / path-traversal class bug.
ci_summary: buildkite pass (Build #72004 passed)

title: node:http/http2 hardening: enforce h2 request pseudo-headers, never report a failed handler as success, and bound/validate the parser paths
author: cirospaciari
reason: Body states "A security review of that branch reported findings" and fixes h2 protocol validation, handler-failure-as-200, and parser bounds in node:http/http2.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-x64-test-bun lane failed, all others pass)

title: fetch: reject non-HTTP(S) schemes before touching the network
author: robobun
reason: Body states fetch of a non-HTTP(S) URL "leaks a DNS query for the scheme name and, if the resolver answers, opens a real connection" — SSRF-adjacent unintended network activity that Node/spec reject with TypeError.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-x64-test-bun lane failed, all others pass)

title: webcore/Blob: validate content-type in the structured-clone deserializer
author: robobun
reason: Body describes that a "crafted serialized image" yields a Blob whose type "propagates unchanged into new Response(blob)'s Content-Type header" including CR/LF control bytes — HTTP header injection via deserialization.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (2 test lanes: alpine-x64-baseline, darwin-14-x64) + cargo clippy fail

title: http(h3): reject DATA before final response HEADERS in the HTTP/3 client
author: robobun
reason: Fixes "Unbounded buffering before the final HEADERS (DoS)" where "a hostile server could send HEADERS(:status 100) followed by an unbounded stream of DATA frames" with no cap.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14 aarch64 + x64 test lanes only; all linux/alpine/debian/darwin-26 pass)

title: node:dgram: honor receiveBlockList and sendBlockList options
author: robobun
reason: IP blocklist security control fails open: "filter object was constructed and silently inert: datagrams from blocked sources were delivered".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (7 test lanes: debian-13, ubuntu-25.04, windows-x64-baseline)

title: node:tls: abort handshake with fatal alert when server rejects client cert
author: robobun
reason: mTLS server with rejectUnauthorized:true "would let the TLS handshake run to completion" so the client sees secureConnect and a clean close instead of a fatal alert on an untrusted cert.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (5 test lanes: darwin-x64, debian x64/aarch64/baseline, ubuntu x64)

title: node:tls: make Server.setSecureContext() rebuild the live TLS context
author: robobun
reason: Certificate rotation is a silent no-op: "the server kept serving its original certificate until the process restarted... the first symptom was an expired-certificate outage weeks later."
ci_summary: buildkite fail (8 test lanes across darwin/debian/ubuntu/windows)

title: usockets: stop reusePort from setting SO_REUSEADDR on Windows TCP listeners
author: robobun
reason: On Windows SO_REUSEADDR "lets any local process bind the same port and receive the original listener's new connections" — a port-hijack vulnerability.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (2 alpine test lanes only; all windows lanes pass)

title: crypto: throw when BN_generate_prime_ex fails in generatePrime
author: robobun
reason: Body says on failure "the caller gets back a full-width random number that looks exactly like a prime but has never been primality-tested" — silent crypto correctness failure returning composites as primes.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (5 test lanes: alpine aarch64/x64/x64-baseline, darwin-14 aarch64/x64)

title: ws: respect client maxPayload and handshakeTimeout options
author: robobun
reason: Silently dropping maxPayload/handshakeTimeout means "code that relies on these as a memory-DoS guard or stalled-handshake guard... got no signal that the guard was gone".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (TypeScript types check + darwin-x64 and darwin-aarch64 test lanes)

title: Bun.password: reject argon2 cost options that verify would refuse
author: robobun
reason: Body shows "out-of-i32-range options silently wrap mod 2^32 into near-minimum cost" for argon2 password hashing, and hash() can mint PHC strings that verify() refuses — a crypto/password-strength weakness.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14-x64 test lane only); TypeScript types check fail

title: install: validate bun.lockb slice descriptors and ids at load time
author: robobun
reason: "A corrupt or crafted bun.lockb crashes bun" with out-of-range panic in release builds; untrusted lockfile input reaches unchecked slice indexing.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only alpine-3.23 x64 + x64-baseline test lanes; all other platforms pass)

title: Bun.password: validate async hash/verify args like the sync forms
author: robobun
reason: Without this, "await Bun.password.hash(req.body.password) with a missing field would silently store a hash of 'undefined'" — an auth footgun best shipped at a version boundary since it changes resolve-to-throw.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (aggregate only; all individual test lanes pass)

title: node:http: wire server socket/response setTimeout to the native inactivity timer
author: robobun
reason: Per-connection setTimeout is a silent no-op so "the canonical slowloris / slow-client mitigation in Node.js" does nothing and "the stalled connection is held open indefinitely with no error and no event".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-aarch64-test-bun lane failed, all other platforms pass)

title: url: preprocess host/hostname setter input so IDNA matches the constructor
author: robobun
reason: Host validation bypass vector: "a filter that validates `new URL(x)` and then assigns `u.hostname = x` validates a different host than it uses", minting A-labels with embedded control characters.
ci_summary: buildkite pass (Build #70498 passed)

title: url: reject invalid Punycode (xn--) labels in special-scheme hosts
author: robobun
reason: Body explicitly states "This is a parser differential on the host, the security-relevant component of a URL: anything that validates a URL with Bun's URL class... (allowlists, SSRF filters, link sanitizers, webhook validators) disagrees with every conformant parser."
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-26-aarch64-test-bun failed, all other lanes pass)

title: http: reject requests with multiple Host header field lines (RFC 9112 5.4)
author: robobun
reason: Body explicitly states the "split-brain authority is the host-header-confusion / cache-poisoning shape" that RFC 9112's MUST exists to prevent.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14-aarch64 expired, darwin-14-x64 exit 2; all other lanes pass)

title: glob: stop bracket expressions from matching path separators in match()
author: robobun
reason: Path-traversal containment bypass: "`uploads/[!.]*` is meant to be confined to `uploads/` but matches `uploads/../secret`".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (2 lanes: alpine-aarch64 test, darwin-14-x64 test; rest pass)

title: node:vm: enforce timeout as a wall-clock deadline that interrupts Atomics.wait
author: robobun
reason: Sandbox timeout bypass: "`timeout` is the whole point of running untrusted code through `node:vm`, so a guest can currently evade the budget entirely with one `Atomics.wait`"; also triggers an ASSERTION FAILED abort on the next evaluation.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (3 lanes: alpine-x64, alpine-x64-baseline, darwin-14-x64; most lanes pass incl. asan/debian/aarch64)

title: dns: normalize legacy IPv4 literals with inet_aton semantics
author: robobun
reason: Body explicitly states "Leading-zero-octet ambiguity is the canonical SSRF / allowlist-bypass vector (CVE-2021-29418, CVE-2021-28918)" — resolving `0177.0.0.1` to a public host instead of loopback is a security bypass.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (1 darwin-26-aarch64 test failure + ~11 expired build jobs; most test lanes pass)

title: node:http2: tie the stream receive window to what the JS readable consumes
author: robobun
reason: Unbounded memory / DoS vector: "no inbound flow control... Any server can push tens of MB per second into a client that is not reading."
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-26-aarch64-test-bun lane failed, all other lanes pass)

title: node:crypto: report DH_check() results the way OpenSSL 3 does
author: robobun
reason: Diffie-Hellman validation is broken both ways: "createDiffieHellman(32) is accepted and keys off a 32-bit 'prime'" (weak params allowed) and the documented hardening pattern "if (dh.verifyError) throw" falsely rejects every standard IETF group.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (1 lane: ubuntu-25.04-x64-baseline expired; all others pass)

title: Reject Bun.serve response header values that cannot be written as an HTTP field-value
author: robobun
reason: Header-injection surface: "Bun.serve writes response header values containing C0 control octets and DEL... onto the wire verbatim" allowing "any handler reflecting an unvetted string into a header" to emit RFC-invalid responses.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (6 lanes Expired infra, 1 darwin-26-aarch64 test fail; most lanes pass)

title: postgres: validate binary datum length and range before decoding
author: robobun
reason: Untrusted-input hardening: "A lying peer could inject data that no query produced, indistinguishable from a real column value" via MitM/proxy on the wire.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin lanes only: 2 expired, 1 darwin-26-aarch64 test failure); all linux/windows/musl pass

title: node:http2: don't kill the process on a malformed HEADERS block sent to a server
author: robobun
reason: Body states "Any peer that can open a TCP connection to an h2/h2c Bun server can trigger it" and "kills the whole process" — a remote DoS and "a regression on main".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (2 darwin-aarch64 lanes: one expired, one test failure; all other platforms pass)

title: tls: don't request client certificates just because a ca was supplied
author: robobun
reason: TLS/mTLS weakness: body states a server with `ca` "broke every ordinary client while still admitting any certificate from any issuer" — effectively an auth bypass for the intended mTLS gate.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14-aarch64 expired, darwin-26-aarch64 test failure; all other lanes pass)

title: Reject interior null bytes in Bun.mmap, Glob.scan, and FileSystemRouter paths
author: robobun
reason: NUL-byte path truncation: "the OS operates on a different, shorter path than the one JS-level code saw and validated" — classic path validation bypass.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-aarch64 lane expired; all others pass)

title: install: hash patch contents with SHA-1 instead of Wyhash11
author: robobun
reason: Fixes "patchedDependencies cache-poisoning" where Wyhash11 collisions let one project's patch be served as another's from the shared cache.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (4 lanes expired/infra, 1 darwin-26-aarch64 test failure)

title: node:https: enforce server handshakeTimeout
author: robobun
reason: Body states an unfinished TLS handshake "was kept open indefinitely, holding a socket and BoringSSL state bounded only by the fd limit (a slowloris against the handshake)" — a resource-exhaustion DoS.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (2 darwin lanes expired, 1 darwin-26-aarch64 test fail; all linux/alpine/debian/asan pass)

title: node:https: fail closed when createServer has no key or cert
author: robobun
reason: Body states https.createServer with no key/cert "serves plaintext HTTP on the 'https' listener" and "turns an intended https endpoint into a working cleartext http endpoint" — a fail-open TLS downgrade vulnerability.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (2 darwin-aarch64 test lanes failed; 3 aarch64 lanes expired/infra; all other lanes pass)

title: net: strict canonical address parsing for SocketAddress and BlockList
author: robobun
reason: BlockList bypass: "false negative on a blocked address" and "a blocklist built from non-canonical text silently guards a different address than any connection uses."
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-26-aarch64 test fail; 3 build jobs expired/infra)

title: tls: apply sessionTimeout to the server's SSL context
author: robobun
reason: TLS sessionTimeout is "validated and then discarded" so "tickets a server mints stay resumable for hours regardless of what the application configured, and an expired one coming back is still accepted" — a session-lifetime security control is silently ineffective.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (mostly expired/infra jobs; 1 real test fail on darwin-26-aarch64, other test lanes pass)

title: node:tls: honor the crl option when building a SecureContext
author: robobun
reason: CRL option silently dropped so "a client certificate revoked by the supplied CRL was fully authorized (authorized === true)" — revoked certs accepted.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-x64-test-bun, exit status -1 infra failure; all other lanes pass)

title: http: reject DEL (0x7F) in request header field values
author: robobun
reason: HTTP parser differential where "two hops in a chain disagree on whether the request is valid"; tests added to request-smuggling.test.ts.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-x64-test-bun failed, all other lanes pass)

title: Bun.SQL(postgres): send plaintext startup when sslmode=prefer and server declines TLS
author: robobun
reason: Fixes both a connection hang ("the connection idles until connectionTimeout") and a security hole: the old `continue` dispatching trailing bytes "is the message-injection shape libpq hardened against in CVE-2021-23222".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14-aarch64 expired, darwin-26-aarch64 test failure; all other lanes pass)

title: node:http2: handle an oversized request header list as a stream error, not a process crash
author: robobun
reason: Remotely-triggerable DoS/crash: an oversized header list "kills the whole node:http2 server process with an uncatchable ERR_HTTP2_STREAM_ERROR. Any peer that can open a TCP connection can trigger it".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-26-aarch64-test-bun lane failed; all other builds/tests passed)

title: sql(postgres): reject duplicate authentication requests
author: robobun
reason: "A server (or active MITM...) drives Bun into an unbounded 100% CPU authentication loop, pre-auth" with "connectionTimeout never fires" — pre-auth DoS/hang exploitable over the wire.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14 aarch64+x64 test lanes; all other platforms pass)

title: webcrypto: reject RSA modulusLength values that cannot be generated exactly
author: robobun
reason: Crypto weakness: "a caller with a policy-mandated size such as 2000 bits was getting weaker RSA than it asked for, with no error" — silently generates weaker-than-requested RSA keys.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (13 lanes Expired — infra timeout, no real test failures; lanes that ran passed)

title: node:url: treat ws: and wss: as slashed protocols
author: robobun
reason: Host-confusion / allow-list bypass risk: resolving a relative reference against a ws: base "replaces the host with a segment of that relative path," and "allow-lists and routers keyed on url.parse(x).host also see hostnames node would never produce."
ci_summary: buildkite fail (all failures are Expired infra jobs; all executed jobs passed)

title: url: stop running url.parse() hostnames through the WHATWG host parser
author: robobun
reason: Body explicitly notes "Hostname allow-lists can be bypassed or broken by the hex/octal/dword rewrite" and a "reachable-input throw" — allow-list bypass is an SSRF-class concern.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14-aarch64 expired, darwin-26-aarch64 test fail; all other lanes pass)

title: tls: reject secureProtocol combined with minVersion or maxVersion
author: robobun
reason: TLS weakness: "the minVersion floor is silently dropped... negotiated the legacy version with no error and nothing in the logs."
ci_summary: buildkite overall fail but all individual lanes pass

title: webcrypto: validate the OKP JWK x parameter against d on import
author: robobun
reason: Missing RFC 8037 validation lets a JWK with mismatched x/d import successfully so "two consumers of the same JWK silently end up with different public keys" — a crypto validation weakness.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (aggregate only; all 74 sub-lanes pass)

title: url: validate percent-decoding and platform path rules in fileURLToPath
author: robobun
reason: Path-validation bypass: "Returning \"\" instead of throwing turns a malformed URL into 'the current directory' for whatever fs call receives it" and unvalidated encoded \\ / characters pass through.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (aggregate only); all individual build/test lanes pass

title: node:tls: apply TLS 1.3 cipher names and honorCipherOrder on servers
author: robobun
reason: TLS server silently ignores cipher configuration: "honorCipherOrder was read nowhere" and TLS 1.3 cipher lists fail or are dropped, so servers cannot enforce their cipher policy — a TLS/crypto weakness.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (aggregate only; all 75 sub-lanes pass)

title: shell: scope `FOO=bar cmd` prefix assignments to that command
author: robobun
reason: Prefix env assignments leak to every later command: "promoting it to a script-wide export leaks it into every later child... curl inherits AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY; under bash it does not."
ci_summary: buildkite fail (aggregate only; all individual lanes pass)

title: bundler: keep a "__proto__" export name an own property in generated exports
author: robobun
reason: Bundler emits bare __proto__ keys so "the key is dropped and the prototype is replaced" — a prototype-pollution vector in generated bundle output.
ci_summary: buildkite pass (build #68306)

title: ci: ignore image publish and windows signing tags on fork PRs
author: alii
reason: Prevents supply-chain attack where a fork PR author controls commit subject and could "enable privileged pipeline steps: baking/publishing the live CI base images and running the Windows code-signing step."
ci_summary: buildkite pass (Build #68074 passed)

title: ws: enforce WebSocketServer maxPayload option
author: robobun
reason: Resource-limit bypass / DoS vector: "`maxPayload` option was ignored under Bun: a frame larger than `maxPayload` was delivered to the `message` handler instead of being rejected."
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-aarch64-test-bun lane failed, all others pass)

title: node:url: return "" from domainToASCII/domainToUnicode for invalid domains
author: robobun
reason: These functions are "the hostname *validation* API" and under Bun "every invalid domain 'succeeds', so allowlist and normalization code that checks the return value gets no failure signal."
ci_summary: buildkite fail (all test-bun lanes failing across linux/darwin/windows/alpine)

title: node:http: enforce server headersTimeout and requestTimeout
author: Jarred-Sumner
reason: headersTimeout/requestTimeout were stored but never enforced, so "a connection with stalled request headers... or that never sent a byte was kept open indefinitely", enabling Slowloris-style resource-exhaustion DoS against node:http servers.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (all test lanes failing across linux/darwin/windows)

title: sql: reject a MySQL prepare-OK carrying statement_id 0
author: robobun
reason: Body states "a compromised or desynced MySQL-compatible server or proxy can trigger it with a single frame" and "the very next execute aborts the process: panic: statement is not prepared" — attacker-controllable DoS/panic.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only alpine-3.23-x64-test-bun lane failed, all others pass)

title: ci: drop gh api from the triage commands' allowed tools
author: alii
reason: Hardens automated triage against prompt injection: commands "run automatically against inbound issues and PRs... with a repo-scoped token" and had overly broad `gh api` grant allowing "arbitrary REST calls with that token".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (aggregate only; all 60 individual lanes pass)

title: bun:ffi: coerce JSCallback return values to the declared return type
author: robobun
reason: Body states "the integer cases leak heap pointer material (ASLR-relevant) to whatever native code consumes the callback's result" and also crashes on debug builds with ASSERTION FAILED.
ci_summary: buildkite aggregate fail but all individual lanes pass

title: Normalize the request-target path before matching Bun.serve routes
author: robobun
reason: Route/auth bypass via raw request-target path traversal: "Handlers attached to a route...can be skipped by the raw spelling" using `/admin/../admin/x`, `%2e`, or backslash spellings to reach a different handler than request.url names.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (overall marked failed, but all 75 individual lanes pass)

title: Bun.serve websocket: don't alias pub/sub topics that differ only in lone surrogates
author: robobun
reason: Body states "any application that builds topic names from user-controlled input ... can be made to receive, or have its publishes delivered to, a topic it never named by injecting a lone surrogate" — a cross-topic message-delivery/authorization bypass.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (cargo clippy + darwin-14 aarch64/x64 tests)

title: node:https: apply the full TLS option set when creating a Server
author: Jarred-Sumner
reason: TLS options like minVersion/maxVersion/ciphers were silently dropped and "a pfx- or secureContext-only configuration was treated as plain HTTP", meaning an HTTPS server could silently serve unencrypted traffic.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14-aarch64 test lane) + Format check fail; all other lanes pass

title: patch: refuse symlink and hard link escapes when applying patches
author: robobun
reason: Fixes a path-traversal / arbitrary-write vulnerability: "applying a patch writes the patch's hunk bytes through it into any file the user can write" via symlink or hard link escapes.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-aarch64 test lane, exit status 2; all other lanes pass)

title: node:crypto: validate OKP private JWK x matches d in createPrivateKey
author: robobun
reason: Body notes "A maliciously inconsistent private JWK is supposed to be rejected at the trust boundary; silently repairing it" — crypto key validation bypass vs RFC 8037.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-26-aarch64 + windows-aarch64 test fails; several lanes Expired/infra; most test lanes pass)

title: Bun.serve: use the absolute-form request-target's authority for request.url
author: robobun
reason: Body explicitly cites "host-confusion chains (cache poisoning, vhost/origin-check bypass)" and reverts a recent regression from #31495 that violated RFC 9112 MUST.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14 aarch64/x64 expired, darwin-26-aarch64 test failure; all linux/alpine/debian/freebsd/windows-build lanes pass)

title: dns.lookup: return every hosts-file address family instead of only the first line's
author: robobun
reason: Body highlights "an A pinned.test query was actually sent to the configured nameserver, so a hostname that was pinned locally leaked to the upstream resolver" — DNS leak of locally-pinned hostnames.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (5 lanes Expired - infra; all actual test jobs passed)

title: Harden bun.exe against DLL search-order hijacking on Windows
author: robobun
reason: Fixes DLL search-order hijacking where "a DLL named after one of bun's system dependencies, dropped next to bun.exe... is loaded in place of the real one".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (8 test lanes: alpine/debian/ubuntu/darwin aarch64+x64, windows-aarch64)

title: patch: reject paths with embedded NUL in is_safe_patch_path
author: robobun
reason: Embedded-NUL path truncation lets a crafted patch write to the wrong file; "reachable from bun install via a crafted patchedDependencies entry".
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14-x64-test-bun, windows-11-aarch64-test-bun; all other lanes pass)

title: Bun.serve(http3): fix heap use-after-free when client resets a streaming response
author: robobun
reason: "Fixes a remotely-triggerable heap-use-after-free in the HTTP/3 server" when a client resets a stream mid-response.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (2 lanes: alpine-x64-baseline, darwin-14-x64; rest pass incl ASAN)

title: node:vm: allocate dynamic-import rejection error in the vm realm
author: robobun
reason: Sandbox escape: "err.constructor.constructor('return process')(); // returns the host process (escapes the vm)" via dynamic-import rejection error allocated in host realm.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14/26-aarch64 test lanes only; all other platforms pass)

title: node:vm: throw defineProperty TypeErrors in the vm realm
author: robobun
reason: Sandbox escape: "err.constructor.constructor('return process')() leaks the host process" — TypeError allocated in host realm lets vm code reach host Function constructor.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14-aarch64 + darwin-26-aarch64 test lanes only; all other platforms pass)

title: worker_threads: honor --no-addons in execArgv after value-taking flags
author: robobun
reason: Security-relevant sandbox flag silently ignored: "--no-addons ... never sees it ... so process.dlopen is not disabled"; a Rust-port regression from the Zig behavior.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (only darwin-14-aarch64-test-bun failed, all other lanes pass)

title: css: bound light-dark() origin expansion depth in relative colors
author: robobun
reason: Exponential O(2^depth) CSS minifier output ("~25 levels reached gigabytes") described as "A DoS vector for anything minifying attacker-supplied CSS."
ci_summary: buildkite fail (3 darwin test lanes only; all linux/asan/windows pass)

title: Fix AES-GCM output when OPENSSL_ia32cap masks AES-NI but not VAES
author: robobun
reason: AES-GCM produces "deterministic garbage ciphertext" whose "tag still verifies" — "silently wrong crypto is the worst possible failure mode", affecting node:crypto and WebCrypto.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (4 test lanes: darwin-14 aarch64/x64, debian-13 x64-baseline, ubuntu-25.04 x64-baseline)

title: css: budget selector expansion and nesting substitution by bytes
author: robobun
reason: Fixes a fuzzer-found CSS minifier OOM where "30 KB in -> 265,879,552 bytes out, 581 MB RSS" — an attacker-controlled input-amplification DoS.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (all test-bun lanes failing across linux/darwin/windows)

title: archive: contain extraction writes within the destination root
author: Jarred-Sumner
reason: Prevents archive path-traversal via pre-existing symlinks: "extracting an archive containing `pivot/file.txt` into a destination that already has `pivot -> <outside dir>` leaves the outside directory empty" — classic Zip-Slip/symlink escape hardening.
ci_summary: buildkite pass

title: node:vm: allocate prepareStackTrace call sites in the vm realm
author: robobun
reason: Sandbox escape: "sites.constructor.constructor('return process')() reached the host process" — host process reachable from inside node:vm context via prepareStackTrace call sites.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (2 darwin test lanes: darwin-14-x64, darwin-26-aarch64); all linux/alpine/debian/ubuntu/freebsd/windows builds+tests pass

title: install: skip symlink entries in extracted tarballs (matching npm) and harden extraction with O_NOFOLLOW
author: alii
reason: Path traversal: a crafted tarball with NFC/NFD-aliased entries "can therefore create a symlink under one normalization and then mkdirat/symlinkat through it... creating directories/symlinks outside the extraction root".
ci_summary: buildkite pass (Build #58660 passed)

title: fix(cron): write macOS launchd logs to ~/Library/Logs, not /tmp
author: alii
reason: Body explicitly cites "/tmp is world-writable and the log path is fully predictable... another local user can pre-create a symlink... (CWE-59 / CWE-377)" — a symlink attack vulnerability fix.
ci_summary: buildkite pass (Build #59257 passed)

title: Only reconstruct file-backed Blobs from in-process structured clone
author: Jarred-Sumner
reason: Deserializing caller-supplied bytes reconstructed path/fd-backed Blobs where "the embedded path or descriptor cannot be assumed to refer to something the caller should be handed a handle to" — arbitrary file/fd access via v8.deserialize/IPC.
ci_summary: buildkite fail (darwin-14-aarch64 expired; all other lanes pass)

title: install: pin a computed sha512 for npm packages whose manifest has no usable integrity
author: Jarred-Sumner
reason: Supply-chain integrity gap: npm packages with missing/unparseable integrity "were recorded in the lockfile with an empty integrity and never verified on any install".
ci_summary: buildkite pass (build #58769)

title: Tighten ffi pointer bounds, sparse archive extraction, and the Windows default trust store
author: Jarred-Sumner
reason: Three hardening fixes: ffi "negative or oversized offset produced a pointer outside the buffer", sparse-tar DoS "a small archive can no longer expand to many GB on disk", and Windows TLS no longer trusting user-creatable `C:\etc\ssl`.
ci_summary: buildkite pass

title: install: preserve empty trustedDependencies across install + lockfile round-trip
author: robobun
